summary: Epic Scarlet>Touch>Fire and soaring Dark Star, tight second set
blurb: The second set erupts with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Fire on the Mountain sandwich and a high‑energy Space → The Wheel segue. Highlights include an epic Dark Star encore and standout renditions of Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Phil lays down a booming bass foundation.
review: July 13, 1984 at Berkeley's Greek Theatre delivered one of the early ’80s’ most memorable nights. After a solid first set that built momentum with The Greatest Story Ever Told and Hell In A Bucket, the band launched into a second‑set masterpiece. Scarlet Begonias flowed seamlessly into Touch of Grey and Fire on the Mountain, a sequence reviewers called “scorching” and “blazing.” From there the groove deepened through Man Smart, Woman Smarter into an inspired Drums → Space → The Wheel stretch where Brent’s keyboards added trippy color. The set closed with tight renditions of Stella Blue and Sugar Magnolia before the encore erupted in a legendary Dark Star that showcased Jerry’s magical lead work despite some vocal strain. Phil’s bass was booming and steady, Bill and Mickey drove the improvisations with fresh dynamics, and Bob’s rhythm guitar locked tightly with the groove. Overall, the performance is hailed as “magical,” “high‑energy,” and one of the strongest shows of the early ’80s.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across the majority of recordings: multiple reviewers praise the Scarlet>Touch>Fire sequence, Dark Star encore, and overall magical vibe. Only a few mixed or negative notes focus on recording mix issues, not performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, beautiful audio and no drop‑outs; showcases the epic Dark Star and scorching Scarlet>Touch>Fire sequence in full clarity.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Touch of Grey → Fire on the Mountain sandwich
Epic Dark Star encore that caps a high‑energy second set
Space → The Wheel segue highlighted by Brent’s trippy keyboards and Mickey’s bell work
Tight rhythm section – Phil's booming bass and Bill & Mickey’s dynamic drumming

band_performance.Jerry: Rare 1984 appearance shines; guitar is raging and magical on Dark Star and the Scarlet>Touch>Fire sequence, with powerful tone despite occasional vocal strain.
band_performance.Phil: Booming, tight bass provides a solid groove that underpins the second‑set jams and drives the overall momentum.
band_performance.Bob: Precise whammy‑bar work and crisp rhythm guitar, especially notable on The Greatest Story Ever Told and in lockstep with Phil’s bass.
band_performance.Brent: Fully developed keyboards add depth and trippy texture, dominating the Space → Wheel segment and enriching the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Dynamic drumming highlighted by expressive bell work on The Wheel; adds fresh energy throughout the set.
band_performance.Billy: Solid, driving drum work that meshes with Mickey to propel the improvisations, especially during the high‑octane second set.
